entries.intro: {npc_name} has been in a foul mood all week. I'm steering clear.
details: Snapped at me over nothing yesterday. I asked a simple question and got my head bitten off.
Other people have noticed too. Nobody wants to be the one to ask what's wrong.
reactions: Whatever it is, I hope they sort it out soon.

statCheck.pass: I think it started after the argument with the trader. That's when the mood shifted.
statCheck.fail: No idea what set it off. Could be anything.

entries.intro: Say what you will about {npc_name}, but that's someone who shows up when it matters.
details: Last month when the storm knocked a tree across the road, they were out there clearing it before anyone else had their boots on.
Never makes a big deal about it either. Just does the work and goes home.
reactions: We could use more people like that around here.

statCheck.pass: I think they learned it growing up rough. When you've had hard times, you don't wait for someone else to step in.
statCheck.fail: I don't know what drives them. Some people are just built that way, I guess.

entries.intro: I don't trust {npc_name}. Something about the way they talk in circles.
details: Ask a straight question, you get three answers that all sound different but say nothing.
I've seen them be perfectly friendly to someone's face and then roll their eyes the moment they turn around.
reactions: I keep things polite. But I keep my distance too.

statCheck.pass: I've caught them in two different stories about where they came from. The details don't line up.
statCheck.fail: I can't point to anything specific. It's just a feeling.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have been getting along well lately. Nice to see.
details: They were working together on something behind the storehouse yesterday. Looked like they were having a good time.
Used to barely speak to each other. I don't know what changed, but it's a welcome improvement.
reactions: Makes the whole place feel a bit lighter when people aren't at each other's throats.

entries.intro: {npc_name} borrowed a lantern from me two months ago. I'm starting to think it's gone for good.
details: I've mentioned it twice. Both times I got a nod and a 'right, right, I'll bring it by.' Still waiting.
It's not even about the lantern anymore. It's the principle.
reactions: Next time I'll just say no.

statCheck.pass: I've noticed they do this with everyone. Borrow something small, never return it, then act surprised when you bring it up.
statCheck.fail: Maybe I'm reading too much into it. People forget things.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me a joke the other day that I'm still thinking about.
details: I won't repeat it because I'd butcher the delivery. Timing is everything with that one.
Didn't expect it at all. We were just talking about the weather and then out of nowhere they had me in stitches.
reactions: Good to laugh like that. Doesn't happen often enough.

entries.intro: You'd think the {npc_role} would keep better hours. {npc_name}'s never around when you actually need them.
details: I went by three times last week. Closed every time.
Other folk have said the same. It's getting to be a pattern.
reactions: I don't need much, but I need to be able to count on the basics.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has a way of making everyone around them feel welcome. It's a rare thing.
details: Even strangers warm up fast. There's just something easy about the way they talk to people.
I've seen them remember small details about folk they've only met once. Names, what they were carrying, where they said they were headed.
reactions: People like that hold a place together without even trying.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been in the foulest mood lately. Snapped at me over absolutely nothing.
details: I said good morning and got a grunt that could strip paint.
Apparently it's been going on for days. I'm not the only one avoiding that end of the path.
reactions: Some people carry their bad days around like a sack of rocks. Best to give them room.

statCheck.pass: I think it started after the roof leak. Spent three nights mopping up water: that'd sour anyone.
statCheck.fail: No idea what set it off. Not my place to pry.

entries.intro: I can never quite tell what {npc_name} is thinking. Friendly enough, but hard to read.
details: We talked for a while the other day and I still couldn't figure out if they were enjoying it or just being polite.
Some people are just private like that. Doesn't mean anything's wrong.
reactions: I don't mind it. Not everyone has to be an open book.

entries.intro: I saw {npc_name} helping {npc_name_2} carry lumber this morning. Didn't even have to be asked.
details: Just walked over, grabbed the other end, and got on with it. Not a word exchanged.
That's the kind of thing people don't notice, but it keeps a place running.
reactions: More of that and less of the bickering, if you ask me.

entries.intro: {npc_name} keeps giving advice nobody asked for. It's becoming a problem.
details: I was mending a boot and got a ten-minute lecture on stitching technique. I've been mending boots for years.
It's not mean-spirited, just exhausting. Every task turns into a lesson.
reactions: I've started timing my errands to miss them. Terrible, but effective.

statCheck.pass: I think it comes from loneliness more than arrogance. Hard to be annoyed once you see it that way.
statCheck.fail: I try not to think about it too hard or I'll say something I regret.

entries.intro: Is it just me, or does {npc_name} always look like they know something you don't?
details: There's this half-smile that shows up right before they change the subject. Every time.
I've tried asking directly and I just get a shrug. Maddening.
reactions: Probably nothing. Some people just have that kind of face.

entries.intro: {npc_name} cooked for a few of us last night. Best meal I've had in weeks.
details: Some kind of roasted root dish with herbs I couldn't name. Simple, but perfect.
We sat around the table long after the food was gone, just talking. Felt like old times.
reactions: A shared meal does more for people than most things.

entries.intro: I don't think {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} have spoken in a week. Something's off between them.
details: They used to walk past each other with a nod at least. Now it's just silence.
Nobody knows what happened and neither of them is talking about it.
reactions: It'll blow over. These things usually do eventually.

statCheck.pass: If I had to guess, it's about the shared plot on the south side. They've disagreed about planting before.
statCheck.fail: I'm not getting in the middle of it. That's how you lose two friends at once.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is one of those people I could go either way on. Some days we get along, some days we don't.
details: It depends entirely on the mood we're both in when we cross paths. No real pattern to it.
We had a perfectly fine conversation yesterday and an awkward silence the day before.
reactions: Not everyone clicks neatly. That's just how people work.

entries.intro: I don't care what anyone says about {npc_name}: I think highly of them. Always have.
details: People talk because {npc_name} doesn't do things the way everyone expects. That's not a flaw in my book.
When I needed help last winter, {npc_name} showed up without being asked. That tells you everything.
reactions: I'd rather have one person like that than ten who only help when it's convenient.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has this habit of humming while working. Same tune, over and over, all day long.
details: I don't think {npc_name} even knows they're doing it. I asked once and got a blank look.
It's not unpleasant exactly. It's just that now I catch myself humming it too, and I don't know the song.
reactions: Funny how someone else's habit can get into your head without you noticing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} seems different lately, quieter somehow. I can't put my finger on what changed.
details: Used to be you couldn't walk past without getting roped into a conversation. Now it's a nod and that's it.
I don't think anything bad happened. Sometimes people just go inward for a while.
reactions: I hope it's just a phase. Everybody has stretches like that.

statCheck.pass: I've noticed {npc_name} spending more time alone in the evenings. Could be working through something personal.
statCheck.fail: I couldn't tell you what's going on. I just notice the difference.

entries.intro: People give {npc_name} a hard time, but I've never had a problem with them.
details: Sure, {npc_name} can be blunt. But at least you always know where you stand.
Half the complaints I hear are from people who got honest answers they didn't want.
reactions: I'll take blunt over polite dishonesty any day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} never cleans up after using the common workspace. Tools left out, shavings everywhere.
details: I've brought it up twice and both times {npc_name} looked surprised, like the mess was invisible.
Someone else always ends up tidying. It's become this unspoken chore that rotates through whoever's unlucky enough to go in next.
reactions: It's a small thing. But small things add up when it's every single day.

entries.intro: I watched {npc_name} work the other day and I was genuinely impressed. There's real skill there.
details: Steady hands, no wasted motion. Everything done in order without having to stop and think about it.
Most people rush or second-guess themselves. {npc_name} just works like breathing.
reactions: I admire people who are good at what they do and don't make a show of it.

statCheck.pass: You can tell by the way {npc_name} holds the tools: loose grip, lets the weight do the work. That takes years to learn.
statCheck.fail: I can see it's good work, even if I couldn't explain what makes it good.

entries.intro: {npc_name} has been telling the same story for a month now. Every time like it's the first time.
details: It's about a fish, or maybe a bird: the details change but the punchline never does.
I've started mouthing the ending along with them. I don't think {npc_name} has noticed yet.
reactions: I don't have the heart to say anything. It makes {npc_name} happy to tell it.

entries.intro: {npc_name} promised to help with the storehouse repairs and never showed. Not a word about it since.
details: I waited half the morning before I gave up and started by myself. Could've been done hours earlier.
It's not the first time either. {npc_name} means well but follow-through isn't a strong point.
reactions: I've stopped counting on it. Easier that way.

statCheck.pass: I think {npc_name} overcommits. Says yes to everything and then can't make good on half of it.
statCheck.fail: Maybe something came up. I don't know what goes on in {npc_name}'s day.

entries.intro: {npc_name} is the most generous person I know, and I mean that in the most exhausting way possible.
details: Every time you see them they're giving you something: food, advice, their opinion on your shoes.
I once told them I didn't need any help and they helped me anyway while I was still talking.
reactions: I appreciate them, I really do. I just need a rest from being appreciated back.

entries.intro: I like {npc_name}. They don't fill every silence with noise.
details: You can sit near them for an hour and nobody says a word and it doesn't feel awkward.
Most people talk because they can't stand quiet. {npc_name} talks because they actually have something to say.
reactions: I respect that more than I can explain.

entries.intro: {npc_name} and I have been arguing about the best way to stack firewood for three years now.
details: They do it bark-side up, I do it bark-side down. Neither of us will budge.
It's become a whole thing. Other people have started taking sides.
reactions: I know it doesn't matter. That's not the point.

statCheck.pass: Bark-side up sheds rain better, but bark-side down dries the inner wood faster. Depends on your climate, honestly.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea which way is actually right. I just picked a side.

entries.intro: I wish {npc_name} and {npc_name_2} would just sit down and talk to each other.
details: They've been avoiding each other for weeks over something neither one will explain to me.
I keep ending up in the middle, carrying messages back and forth like a bird.
reactions: They'd sort it out in five minutes if they'd stop being stubborn.

entries.intro: {npc_name} always looks like they're about to say something important. They never do.
details: They'll open their mouth, pause, then just nod and walk away.
I've started nodding back. It's become our whole conversation.
reactions: Maybe the nod is the important thing. Who knows.

statCheck.pass: Some people process things internally before they speak. It's not shyness: it's deliberation.
statCheck.fail: I can't tell if they're deep or just distracted.

entries.intro: I owe {npc_name} for what they did during the last bad winter. Not everyone stepped up like that.
details: They went door to door checking on people without anyone asking them to.
Never mentioned it afterward either. That's the part that matters.
reactions: You learn who people really are when things get hard.

entries.intro: I can't figure {npc_name} out. Some days they're friendly, other days they look right through you.
details: I said good morning last week and got nothing. The next day they brought me a jar of preserves.
I've stopped trying to predict it. I just take whatever version shows up.
reactions: People are complicated. I'm sure I'm no easier to read.

statCheck.pass: People like that are usually dealing with something they don't talk about. The good days are when they've got energy to spare.
statCheck.fail: I have no idea what's going on with them. I've given up guessing.

entries.intro: {npc_name} told me something the other day that I keep turning over in my head.
details: It wasn't anything profound. Just an offhand remark about how most problems solve themselves if you wait long enough.
I'm not sure I agree, but I haven't been able to stop thinking about it.
reactions: That's the trouble with simple ideas. They stick.
